session.user:
Run the full test suite for this project, excluding the known-failing test:
```bash cd /home/runner/work/erk/erk && uv run pytest tests/ -x -q --ignore=tests/cli/commands/split/test_plan.py 2>&1 | tail -40 ```
Then also run lint and format checks: ```bash cd /home/runner/work/erk/erk && uv run ruff check src/ tests/ packages/ cd /home/runner/work/erk/erk && uv run ruff format --check src/ tests/ packages/ ```
Report ALL results. Do not try to fix anything.
session.assistant: I'll run the test suite and lint checks as requested.

tool_use.param: Run full test suite excluding known-failing test, show last 40 lines

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: uv run ruff check src/ tests/ packages/

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: uv run ruff format --check src/ tests/ packages/

session.assistant:
## Test Results Summary
**pytest: FAILED** - 1 test failure with 2 related issues
The test suite failed on the first failing test:
**File:** `tests/core/test_commit_message_generator.py` **Test:** `test_generate_success` **Line:** 77 **Error:** `AttributeError: 'FakePromptExecutor' object has no attribute 'calls'`
The test attempts to assert `len(executor.calls) == 1`, but the `FakePromptExecutor` fake implementation does not have a `calls` attribute.
**Additional issue on the same file:**
**File:** `tests/core/test_commit_message_generator.py` **Line:** 281 **Violation:** `E501 Line too long (101 > 100)`
```python output="```markdown\nAdd new feature\n\n## Summary\n\nThis adds a new feature.\n```", ```
This line exceeds the 100-character limit by 1 character.
---
**ruff check:** FAILED
Found 1 linting error as shown above (line length violation).
**ruff format:** PASSED
All 1645 files are already correctly formatted.
---
### Summary
- **pytest:** 1 test failure (FakePromptExecutor missing `calls` attribute tracking) - **ruff check:** 1 lint error (line too long) - **ruff format:** All files properly formatted
